title: Complete Guide to Acidity Medicines: Omeprazole vs Pantoprazole — Which is Better?
excerpt: A detailed comparison of the two most popular acidity medicines in India. Learn about their uses, dosage, side effects, and which one your doctor recommends.
content:
Understanding Acidity and Acid Reflux

Acidity, medically known as Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD), is one of the most prevalent health issues affecting millions of Indians. It occurs when the stomach produces excess hydrochloric acid, which then flows back into the esophagus, causing a burning sensation in the chest and throat.

The global pharmaceutical industry has developed several classes of drugs to combat acidity, but the most effective and widely prescribed are Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs). Two of the most popular PPIs available at Adarsh Medical Store, Garhakota, are Omeprazole (sold as Omez) and Pantoprazole (sold as Zovanta by Dr. Reddy's). You can view our complete catalog of Dr. Reddy's products here.

How Do Proton Pump Inhibitors Work?

PPIs work by irreversibly blocking the proton pump in the stomach. Unlike antacids that merely neutralize existing acid, PPIs prevent acid from being produced in the first place, providing longer-lasting and more effective relief.

Omeprazole (Omez) vs Pantoprazole (Zovanta)

ParameterOmeprazolePantoprazole
Speed of ReliefFaster — 30 minModerate — 1-2 hours
Acid Suppression Duration12-17 hours15-24 hours
Drug InteractionsMore — avoid with ClopidogrelFewer — safer choice
Best ForAcute, occasional acidityChronic GERD, long-term use
Price Range₹2-5 per tablet₹5-12 per tablet

title: Cefixime Antibiotic: Complete Guide to Uses, Dosage, and Side Effects
excerpt: Everything you need to know about Cefixime 200mg tablets — one of the most prescribed antibiotics in India for throat, ear, and urinary tract infections.
content:
What is Cefixime?

Cefixime is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic that fights bacteria in the body. It is one of the most widely prescribed oral antibiotics, known for its broad-spectrum activity against both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

At Adarsh Medical Store in Garhakota, Cefixime is available as Cefiwok CV (Cefixime 200mg + Clavulanic Acid 125mg) and Cefiwok-O, both manufactured by Dr. Reddy's Laboratories.

Dosage Guidelines

Patient GroupDosageFrequencyDuration
Adults (above 18 years)200mg tabletTwice daily (every 12 hours)5-7 days
Children (6-12 years)100mg tablet or suspensionTwice daily5 days
Typhoid Fever200mg tabletTwice daily10-14 days

Common Side Effects

Like all medications, Cefixime can cause side effects. The most common is Diarrhea, followed by nausea and abdominal pain. If you experience severe allergic reactions, discontinue use and consult a doctor immediately.

title: Diabetes Management: Metformin and Glimepiride — A Complete Patient Guide
excerpt: Learn how to manage Type 2 Diabetes effectively with the right medications. A comprehensive guide covering Metformin, Glimepiride, diet tips, and monitoring.
content:
Understanding Type 2 Diabetes

Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is a chronic metabolic disorder affecting the body's ability to use insulin. With over 101 million cases, managing diabetes through diet and medication is crucial. The most commonly prescribed oral anti-diabetic drugs are Metformin and Glimepiride.

Metformin vs Glimepiride

Metformin (Glycomet, Obimet) is the first-line treatment. It decreases liver glucose production and increases insulin sensitivity. Glimepiride (Amaryl) is added when Metformin alone isn't enough, working by stimulating the pancreas to release more insulin.

MedicationStarting DoseMaximum DoseWhen to Take
MedicationStarting DoseMaximum DoseWhen to Take
Metformin500mg once daily2000mg/day (divided)With or after meals
Glimepiride1mg once daily8mg/dayWith breakfast
Combination (Met+Glim)500mg/1mg1000mg/4mgWith breakfast

Blood Sugar Monitoring & Diet

Regular monitoring is essential. Choose complex carbohydrates, include high-fiber vegetables, and avoid packaged juices. For patients in the Sagar region, Adarsh Medical Store offers complete diabetes care equipment including glucometers and test strips.

title: Antifungal Creams Guide: Clotrimazole, Ketoconazole, and Managing Skin Infections
excerpt: A comprehensive guide to treating common fungal skin infections including ringworm, athlete's foot, and jock itch with the right antifungal medications.
content:
Fungal Skin Infections in Tropical Climates

Fungal skin infections (dermatophytosis) account for 20-25% of dermatology consultations in India. The humid climate promotes infections like Tinea Corporis (ringworm) and Tinea Pedis (athlete's foot).

Top Antifungal Medicines

Here are the best treatments available at our retail store:

  • Candigurd B Cream (Clotrimazole + Beclometasone): Combats the fungus while reducing itching and redness.
  • Ketoluxe ZPTO Shampoo: Best for severe dandruff and scalp fungal issues.
  • Eclospan Cream: A triple-action formula for mixed bacterial/fungal infections.

Proper Application Technique

  1. Wash with mild soap and pat completely dry.
  2. Apply a pea-sized amount, extending beyond the visible edge.
  3. Apply twice daily and continue for 1-2 weeks after symptoms clear to prevent recurrence.
  • Clotrimazole Cream on Tata 1mg

Contact Adarsh Medical Store Garhakota for wholesale supply of ointments.

title: ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution): The Life-Saving Treatment for Dehydration
excerpt: A detailed guide to understanding ORS — how it works, when to use it, how to prepare it correctly, and why it is considered one of the greatest medical discoveries.
content:
What is Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS)?

Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) is a precisely formulated mixture of glucose and electrolytes designed to treat dehydration rapidly. At Adarsh Medical Store, we stock Rebalanz ORS by Dr. Reddy's — a WHO-approved formula available in multiple flavors.

WHO-Recommended ORS Formula

The current WHO-UNICEF recommended formula produces a solution with an osmolarity of 245 mOsm/L, proven to be highly effective. It is critical for treating diarrheal diseases, heat exhaustion, and high fevers in children.

Dosage Guidelines

Age GroupAmount After Each Loose StoolAmount Per Day
Under 2 years50-100 ml500 ml
2-10 years100-200 ml1000 ml
Above 10 years / Adults200-400 mlAs needed

How to Prepare

Mix one sachet in exactly 1 litre of clean, boiled, and cooled drinking water. Do not add extra sugar or salt. Use within 24 hours.

title: Blood Pressure Medications Explained: Amlodipine, Telmisartan, and How to Control Hypertension
excerpt: A complete guide to understanding high blood pressure medications, how they work, their side effects, and essential lifestyle tips for keeping your BP under control.
content:
Understanding Hypertension

High blood pressure is a silent killer affecting over 20% of the Indian population. Normal pressure is below 120/80 mmHg. Stage 1 hypertension begins at 130/80 mmHg. Regular monitoring using home devices is critical.

Major Classes of Medications

1. Amlodipine (Calcium Channel Blocker): Relaxes blood vessels. Works for 24 hours with a single dose.
2. Telmisartan (ARB): Blocks the hormone that constricts blood vessels. Protects the kidneys and heart.
3. Metoprolol (Beta-Blocker): Slows the heart rate to reduce pumping pressure.

Lifestyle Management

Medication MUST be paired with lifestyle changes: restrict daily sodium to under 5g, exercise for 150 minutes weekly, and practice yoga for stress reduction. You can explore our cardiovascular partner brands.

  • Amlodipine on Tata 1mg
  • BP Medicines on PharmEasy

title: Pain Relief Medicines: Paracetamol vs Ibuprofen vs Diclofenac
excerpt: A practical guide to choosing the right painkiller for headaches, body pain, fever, and inflammation. Understand doses, risks, and when to see a doctor.
content:
Comparing Pain Relievers

Not all painkillers are the same. Choosing the wrong medication can lead to stomach ulcers, liver damage, or kidney issues. Here is a breakdown of the three most common options supplied by Adarsh Medical Stores.

FeatureParacetamolIbuprofenDiclofenac
Pain ReliefMild-ModerateModerateStrong
Anti-inflammatoryNoYesYes (Strong)
Fever ReductionYesYesYes
Stomach SafetySafeModerate riskHigher risk

Safety Rules

  1. Never take two different NSAIDs (like Ibuprofen and Diclofenac) together.
  2. If pain persists beyond 3-5 days, visit a doctor safely.
  3. Paracetamol overdose is extremely dangerous to the liver. Do not exceed 4000mg/day.
  • Paracetamol on Tata 1mg

title: Vitamin D Deficiency: Symptoms, Treatment, and Best Supplements in India
excerpt: Over 70% of Indians are Vitamin D deficient. Learn the symptoms, causes, best supplements, and how to get tested and treated for this widespread health issue.
content:
The Vitamin D Epidemic

Despite abundant sunshine, over 70% of Indians suffer from Vitamin D deficiency. Symptoms include persistent fatigue, frequent bone pain, slow wound healing, and hair loss. Vitamin D is essential not just for bones, but for a robust immune system.

Treatment Protocols

The standard treatment in India is a loading dose of 60,000 IU Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3) once weekly for 8 weeks. Maintenance doses follow thereafter. We provide wholesale Vitamin D supplements from verified partners like Mankind and Macleods.

Level (ng/mL)StatusAction Required
Below 20DeficientImmediate 60,000 IU weekly protocol
20-29InsufficientDaily Supplementation
30-100SufficientMaintenance (Sunlight)
